2604.11703.title: DreamKG: A KG-Augmented Conversational System for People Experiencing Homelessness
2604.11703.paper_summary: People experiencing homelessness (PEH) face substantial barriers to accessing timely, accurate information about community services. DreamKG addresses this through a knowledge graph-augmented conversational system that grounds responses in verified, up-to-date data about Philadelphia organizations, services, locations, and hours. Unlike standard large language models (LLMs) prone to hallucinations, DreamKG combines Neo4j knowledge graphs with structured query understanding to handle location-aware and time-sensitive queries reliably. The system performs spatial reasoning for distance-based recommendations and temporal filtering for operating hours. Preliminary evaluation shows 59% superiority over Google Search AI on relevant queries and 84% rejection of irrelevant queries. This demonstration highlights the potential of hybrid architectures that combines LLM flexibility with knowledge graph reliability to improve service accessibility for vulnerable populations effectively.
2604.11703.paper_summary_zh: 無家可歸者（PEH）面臨著獲取有關社區服務的及時、準確信息的重大障礙。DreamKG 通過一個增強知識圖譜的對話系統來解決這個問題，該系統基於有驗證的、最新的費城組織、服務、地點和營業時間數據來提供回應。與標準的大型語言模型（LLMs）容易出現幻覺不同，DreamKG 將 Neo4j 知識圖譜與結構化查詢理解相結合，以可靠地處理位置感知和時間敏感的查詢。該系統進行基於距離的推薦的空間推理和營業時間的時間過濾。初步評估顯示在相關查詢上比 Google Search AI 提高了 59% 的優越性，並且對不相關查詢的拒絕率達到 84%。這一展示突顯了混合架構的潛力，該架構將 LLM 的靈活性與知識圖譜的可靠性相結合，有效改善脆弱群體的服務可及性。
2604.11703.author: Javad M Alizadeh et.al.
2604.11703.authors: Javad M Alizadeh, Genhui Zheng, Chiu C Tan, Yuzhou Chen, Omar Martinez, Philip McCallion, Ying Ding, Chenguang Yang, AnneMarie Tomosky, Huanmei Wu
